---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

[Download CV (PDF)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/12GEEvpY18VRCxEgb1mrXxdZC5qV4KCir/view){.cv-download target="_blank"}

::: {.cv-section}

### Education

::: {.cv-item}
[2019--2024]{.cv-dates} • **Ph.D. in Computer Science**, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (w/ Prof. Sanmi Koyejo)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2022--2024]{.cv-dates} • **Visiting Ph.D. Student**, Stanford University
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2015--2019]{.cv-dates} • **B.S. with Honors in Mechanical Engineering** (Minors: Mathematics, Computer Science), Texas A&M University
:::

:::


::: {.cv-section}

### Appointments

::: {.cv-item}
[2025--present]{.cv-dates} • AI Center Fellow in Residence, Schmidt Sciences
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2024--present]{.cv-dates} • Postdoctoral Researcher, MIT (Healthy ML Lab, w/ Prof. Marzyeh Ghassemi)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2025--present]{.cv-dates} • Postdoctoral Scholar, Eric and Wendy Schmidt Center, Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
:::

:::


::: {.cv-section}

### Selected Industry & Lab Experience

::: {.cv-item}
[2023--2024]{.cv-dates} • Research Intern, Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems (w/ Dr. Moritz Hardt)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2023]{.cv-dates} • Machine Learning Intern, Cruise LLC
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2022]{.cv-dates} • Student Researcher, Google Brain / Google DeepMind (w/ Dr. Alex D'Amour)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
[2017--2022]{.cv-dates} • R&D Intern, Sandia National Laboratories (w/ Dr. Eric Goodman)
:::

:::


::: {.cv-section}

### Honors & Awards

::: {.cv-item}
Schmidt Sciences AI Center Fellowship • Best Paper, NeurIPS 2025 Workshop on Evaluating the Evolving LLM Lifecycle • NeurIPS 2025 Top Area Chair • NYU Tandon Faculty First-Look Fellow • Georgia Tech FOCUS Fellow • Sloan Scholarship • Beckman Graduate Research Fellowship • GEM Associate Fellowship • NSF Miniature Brain Machinery Traineeship • ICML 2022 Top Reviewer (10%)
:::

:::


::: {.cv-section}

### Service

::: {.cv-item}
**Academic.**\
– *Organizing:* Program Chair, EurIPS Workshop on the Science of Benchmarking and Evaluating AI (2025) • Program Chair, ML4H (2025)\
– *Editing & Area Chairing:* Action Editor, TMLR (2026) • Area Chair, NeurIPS (2024--2026; Top AC 2025) • Area Chair, ICML (2025) • Area Chair, ICML TF2M (2024)\
– *Reviewing:* NeurIPS (2022--2024) • NeurIPS Position Papers (2025) • NeurIPS LLM-eval Workshop (2025) • NeurIPS AFCP Workshop (2022) • NeurIPS BAI Workshop (2021) • ICML (2022--2024; Top Reviewer 2022) • AISTATS (2022) • JMLR (2023--2024) • Nature (2025) • Nature Medicine (2025) • NEJM (2024) • Oxford Handbook of Cognitive Enhancement and Brain Plasticity (2024)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
**MIT.** Organizer, AI for Society Seminar (2024--present) • Organizer, LIDS Conference (2025) • Volunteer, Women in Data Science Worldwide (2025)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
**Stanford.** Mentor, LINXS Program (2024)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
**UIUC.** Executive Board, Black, Indigenous, and Latinx in Tech (2022--2024) • Mentor, Directed Reading Program (2022--2024) • Subcommittee Member, Broadening Participation in Computing (2021--2022) • Affiliate Member, IDEA Institute (2020--2024) • Graduate Student Representative, Graduate Study Committee (2022) • Mentor, Graduates Engineers Diversifying Illinois (2020--2022) • Mentor, DREU (2021)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
**Texas A&M.** Chair, Brown Engineering Honors Program (2018--2019) • President, Robotics Team (2018--2019) • Mentor, Regents Scholar Program (2017--2019)
:::

::: {.cv-item}
**Outreach.** Mentor, Black in AI: Emerging Leaders in AI Program (2024--present) • Mentor, iAAMCS (2023--present)
:::

:::


::: {.cv-section}

### Mentoring

I am happy to mentor students with overlapping research interests. Particularly for undergrads at MIT, programs like [UROP](https://urop.mit.edu/) are a great mechanism for mentorship. I am also very happy to give advice on applying to and navigating graduate programs in computer science, especially for those to whom this type of guidance would be otherwise unavailable.

:::
